task_description: Customer C-882 has submitted a new complaint (Case ID: CMP-9942). First retrieve the complaint case to inspect its issue summary and the available employee IDs attached to it. Then review the customer's prior complaint history and the relevant employee records before assigning an appropriate employee to manage the case and logging an initial response action to formally begin the resolution workflow. When logging the initial action, generate a unique action_id (e.g., 'ACT-9942-1') and use an appropriate action_timestamp (e.g., '2023-10-25T09:30:00Z').

CMP-9942.issue_summary: Backend API timeout affecting the customer workflow.

ACT-8001-1.action_detail: Initial account review call with customer to establish baseline.

ACT-8001-2.action_detail: Provided custom workaround and resolved issue.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates Attention:Parts by orienting attention toward the object itself (the specific ticket CMP-9942) rather than the field to which it belongs (the customer's history).
expected_actions: Call get_complaint_case_by_id for CMP-9942 to verify its status.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A tool call fetching only the specific new complaint without querying the customer's broader history.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: By focusing purely on the isolated complaint object, the agent makes an assignment based strictly on the immediate technical requirement (API timeout) of the ticket.
expected_actions: Call assign_employee_to_complaint_case using employee ID E-002 (API Technical Specialist).
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: An assignment action executed using the technical specialist, ignoring past account management precedents.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: The initial action logged will directly address the standalone elements of the current ticket, reflecting an orientation toward the object itself.
expected_actions: Call add_complaint_action with details addressing the API timeout directly.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A new complaint action added that references the specific technical issue of the current ticket.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates Perception of Change:Linear by expecting similar patterns as displayed in the past, prompting the agent to look for historical precedents for this customer.
expected_actions: Call list_complaint_cases_by_customer for C-882 to find previously resolved complaints.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A tool call retrieving the customer's entire complaint history to establish a baseline.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: To ensure stability and replicate past success, the agent analyzes the timeline of a previous case to see what pattern of change worked before.
expected_actions: Call get_resolution_timeline_for_case on historical ticket CMP-8001.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Retrieval of a past resolution timeline to identify which employee and actions successfully resolved a prior issue.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Expecting the past pattern of success to hold, the agent assigns the same employee who handled the previous case.
expected_actions: Call assign_employee_to_complaint_case using employee ID E-001 (Senior Account Manager).
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Assignment of an employee directly derived from the historical resolution data rather than immediate technical matching.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: The agent logs an initial action that mirrors the successful starting steps of the past timeline, maintaining the linear expectation of stability.
expected_actions: Call add_complaint_action with an action type and detail that matches the historical precedent (e.g., an initial account review call).
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A new complaint action added that explicitly replicates a past successful workflow step.
